title: Learning Analytics
summary: Turning learner data into evidence that instructors and designers can actually act on.
description: We build dashboards, models, and analysis pipelines that surface meaningful patterns in learner behavior, without losing sight of the humans behind the data. Our work spans log-file analysis, engagement modeling, and early-warning systems for student support.

title: Assessment
summary: Designing measures that tell the truth about what learners know and can do.
description: From formative classroom checks to large-scale program assessment, we design, validate, and refine instruments so that assessment results are fair, meaningful, and useful to the people who rely on them.

title: Curriculum Design
summary: Building learning experiences backward from the outcomes that matter.
description: We apply evidence-based instructional design frameworks to build curricula that are coherent, engaging, and grounded in how people actually learn, across K-12, higher education, and workforce contexts.

title: Qualitative Research
summary: Listening closely to the lived experience of learners and educators.
description: Through interviews, ethnography, and document analysis, we surface the stories and structures that numbers alone can't capture, and use them to inform more humane instructional design.

title: Mixed Methods
summary: Combining numbers and narratives for a fuller picture of learning.
description: We design studies that integrate quantitative and qualitative evidence, using each to explain, extend, or challenge the other, in order to produce findings that are both rigorous and grounded in context.

title: Educational Data Science
summary: Applying data science methods to open questions in learning research.
description: We use statistical modeling, text mining, and computational methods to work with large, messy, real-world educational datasets, always in service of a research question, not the other way around.

title: AI in Education
summary: Studying how AI tools are reshaping teaching, learning, and assessment.
description: We research the design, evaluation, and ethical use of AI-enabled learning tools, including intelligent tutoring systems, generative AI in the classroom, and automated feedback systems.

title: Engineering Education
summary: Improving how engineers are taught, trained, and retained.
description: Working closely with engineering departments, we study curriculum, identity, and persistence to help build engineering programs that prepare students for real practice and keep them in the field.

title: Program Assessment
summary: Helping departments and programs show, and improve, their impact.
description: We partner with academic programs to design assessment plans that satisfy accreditation requirements while genuinely informing continuous improvement, not just checking a box.
